title: United States and Iran sign agreement to end hostilities
summary: The memorandum, which includes the demining of the Strait of Hormuz and a reconstruction plan for Iran, is seen as a triumph for Tehran and criticized by Israel.

title: DAZN suspends new annual subscriptions due to misleading advertising
summary: The sports streaming platform will offer refunds to users who requested cancellation after being flagged for confusing billing practices.

title: Nikkei 225 index surpasses 70,000 points for the first time
summary: The milestone comes less than two months after reaching 60,000, driven by market optimism and corporate earnings.

title: Lower House constitutional commission debates reform of Article 9
summary: Political parties continue deliberations on revising the pacifist constitution, with differences between the ruling coalition and the opposition.

title: Democratic Party for the People considers joining the ruling coalition
summary: PDP leader Tamaki expresses willingness to cooperate with the LDP, while Rengo union rejects the expansion of the coalition.

title: Japan's emperor and empress strengthen ties with the Netherlands on official visit
summary: The imperial couple participated in cultural and scientific events, including a visit to the museum where Vermeer's 'Girl with a Pearl Earring' is displayed.

title: Yen falls to 160 yen per dollar amid expectations of U.S. rate hike
summary: The Japanese currency weakens on the prospect that the Federal Reserve will continue to raise interest rates to control inflation.

title: Tuberculosis outbreak in Osaka kills one man and infects 14 others
summary: The victim, aged 40, worked at a company where at least 14 colleagues were infected, according to health authorities.

title: Executives arrested for illegal trafficking of Chinese nannies for wealthy families
summary: The accused allegedly facilitated the irregular entry of domestic workers from China, charging large sums of money from employers.

title: Court ruling in death of pregnant woman struck by vehicle moves family
summary: The verdict allowed the mourners to communicate the outcome to the deceased wife and daughter, bringing relief and tears to those present.

title: Israel cuts ties with EU's top diplomat
summary: The Israeli government accused the European Union of bias in the conflict with Palestinians and decided to suspend all contact with its foreign representative.

title: Kim Yo-jong lashes out at G7 statement on North Korean denuclearization
summary: The sister of North Korea's leader called the G7 position 'the worst destructive choice' and promised a firm response.

title: China conducts maritime surveys east of Taiwan, Taipei protests
summary: The Taiwanese government stated that China is not a coastal state in that area and rejected the unauthorized research activities.

title: Japan launches public-private initiative to boost manga exports
summary: The plan includes training multilingual translators and creating a national digital platform to compete in the global market.

title: Four candidates vie for governorship of Shiga Prefecture
summary: The election, which pits the current governor seeking a fourth term against three opponents, focuses on the economy and the transport tax.

title: ASEAN meets with Putin, prioritizes energy cooperation with Russia
summary: At the summit, Southeast Asian leaders avoided condemning the invasion of Ukraine and sought to strengthen energy ties.

title: Kubo Takefusa catches cold, doubtful for Japan's match against Tunisia
summary: The forward did not participate in training and remains at the hotel recovering, while the team considers options to replace him.

title: Minister Hayashi and former minister Takeda discuss internal situation of LDP
summary: Both politicians met to exchange views on factional dynamics and strategy of the ruling party.

title: Prime Minister's Office corrects parliamentary response on defamatory video
summary: The controversy arose over a video attacking the opposition, leading Bunshun magazine to temporarily suspend its distribution and modify the content.

title: Japanese municipalities under fire for failing to report citizen deaths in a timely manner
summary: In two separate cases, one municipal government left a corpse uncollected for months and another failed to report the death of a welfare recipient.
